problem: Recall that a perfect square is the square of some integer.  How many perfect squares less than 10,000 can be represented as the difference of two consecutive perfect squares?

classified_questions.question: How can we represent a perfect square as the difference of two consecutive perfect squares?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the expression we get when we rewrite the difference of squares as $(b+1-b)(b+1+b)$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What condition must be satisfied in order for a perfect square to be represented as $2b+1$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can every positive odd integer be represented in the form $2b+1$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many odd perfect squares are there from $1^2$ to $99^2$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many perfect squares less than 10,000 can be represented as the difference of two consecutive perfect squares?
classified_questions.label: b
